div.div: Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks
the forward and backward signals can be directly propagated from one block to any other block when using identity mappings as skip connections and after-addition activation.

1. training becomes easier when h(xl)h(x_{l})h(xl​) and
f(yl)f(y_{l})f(yl​) are identity mappings
2. identity mapping h(xl)h(x_{l})h(xl​) achieves the fastest error reduction and lowest training loss
3. a clean information path is helpful for easing optimization.
4. new residual unit: ReLU and BN as pre-activation of the weight layers

The feature xLx_{L}xL​ of any deeper unit LLL can be
any shallower feature xlx_{l}xl​ plus a residual function

xL=x0+∑i=0L−1F(xi,Wi)x_{L}=x_{0} + \sum_{i=0}^{L-1} \mathcal{F} (x_{i}, \mathcal{W}_{i})xL​=x0​+∑i=0L−1​F(xi​,Wi​)
The feature xLx_{L}xL​ is the summation of the outputs of all preceding residual functions plus x0x_{0} x0​
ensures that information is directly propagated back to any shallower unit lll.
∂L∂xL\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_{L}} ∂xL​∂L​ propagates information directly without concerning any weight layers,

∂L∂xL∂∂xl∑i=lL−1F(xi,Wi)\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_{L}} \frac{\partial}{\partial x_{l}} \sum_{i=l}^{L-1} \mathcal{F} (x_{i}, \mathcal{W}_{i})∂xL​∂L​∂xl​∂​∑i=lL−1​F(xi​,Wi​) propagates through the weight layers

semantics.annotation: \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_{l}} =\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_{L}}\frac{\partial x_{L}}{\partial x_{l}} =\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_{L}} \left(1+\frac{\partial}{\partial x_{l}} \sum_{i=l}^{L-1} \mathcal{F} (x_{i}, \mathcal{W}_{i}) \right)

div.div: ∂∂xl∑i=lL−1F(xi,Wi) \frac{\partial}{\partial x_{l}} \sum_{i=l}^{L-1} \mathcal{F} (x_{i}, \mathcal{W}_{i})∂xl​∂​∑i=lL−1​F(xi​,Wi​) cannot be always -1 for all samples in a mini-batch.
gradient of a layer does not vanish even when the weights are arbitrarily small.

Formula:
xL=xl+∑i=lL−1F(xi,Wi)x_{L}=x_{l} + \sum_{i=l}^{L-1} \mathcal{F} (x_{i}, \mathcal{W}_{i})xL​=xl​+i=l∑L−1​F(xi​,Wi​)
for any deeper unit LLL and any shallower unit lll
Disscusion:signal can be directly propagated from any unit to another, both forward and backward.
initialization of the biases bgb_{g}bg​ is critical
with hyper-parameter search on the initial value of bgb_{g}bg​

F\mathcal{F}F scaled by g(x)=Sigmoid(Wgx+bg)g(x) = \text{Sigmoid}(W_{g}x+b_{g})g(x)=Sigmoid(Wg​x+bg​)
h(xl)h(x_{l})h(xl​) scaled by 1−g(x)1- g(x)1−g(x)
initialization of the biases bgb_{g}bg​ is critical

h(xl)h(x_{l})h(xl​) scaled by 1−g(x)1- g(x)1−g(x)
F\mathcal{F}F unchanged

Note :When stacking so many Residual Units ,
even the shortest path may still impede signal propagation.
fropout on the output of the identity shortcut

Dropout statistically imposes a scale of λ\lambdaλ
with an expectation of 0.5 on the shortcut, thus impedes signal propagation.
shortcut connections are the most direct paths for the information to propagate

Multiplicative manipulations on the shortcuts can hamper
information propagation and lead to optimization problems

gating and convolutional shortcuts introduce more parameters,
but no more optimization
degradation of these models is caused by optimization issues, instead of representational abilities.
3 On the Importance of Identity Skip Connections
Branch 1: weight params + BN + ReLU + weight params + BN
Branch 1: weight params + BN + ReLU + weight params
BN impedes clear shortcut information propagation
ReLU leads to a non-negative output on residual
Using asymmetric after-addition activation is equivalent to constructing a pre-activation Residual Unit
pre-activation models are better than the baseline in general (Table 3)
f=f = f= ReLU, the signal is impacted if it is negative and truncation when deep net
f=f = f= identity mapping, signal can be propagated directly and reduce training loss
f=f = f= ReLU not severe when the ResNet has fewer layers
in our pre-activation, the inputs to all weight layers have been normalized
4 On the Usage of Activation Functions

identity shortcut connections and identity after-addition activation
are essential for making information propagation smooth.
Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks
